Dashboard Archive: 2026-05-15 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Long-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns from 1 month to 10 years, with relative performance vs SPY over 1y/5y/10y. Same scoring engine as the short-term ranker but optimized for longer investment horizons and secular trends.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: momentum-ranker-long Today's data reveals an exceptional outlier in LINC, which dominates the ranking at position #1 with a score of 94.4 and extraordinary long-term returns of 2,444% over 10 years compared to SPY's performance, creating a 2,185 percentage point outperformance gap. LINC demonstrates consistent acceleration across timeframes with a 4.23x ratio between 10y and 5y returns, passes all four ratio thresholds, trades above all major moving averages, and shows a clean momentum signal of 0.7 with a strong_buy flag. In contrast, REX ranks second but with more modest long-term credentials (472% over 10 years, only 213% outperformance vs SPY), and shows a concerning negative 99.34% relative performance against QQQ over 10 years despite similar short-term ratio strength. LINC's low correlation to financial sector (XLF at 0.022) and consumer staples (XLP at 0.067) paired with its highest correlation to materials (XLB at 0.336) suggests the momentum is driven by specialized secular trends rather than broad market correlation, which historically tends to be more durable for long-term positioning.
